an3.65:1.2: at one time, the *Bhagavā*, wandering on tour among the Kosalans together with a large *saṅgha* of *bhikkhus*, arrived at Kesamutta, a town of the Kālāmas.
an3.65:1.4: “Indeed, sirs, the ascetic Gotama, son of the Sakyans, gone forth from a Sakyan family, has arrived at Kesamutta.
an3.65:1.5: And concerning that venerable Gotama, such a good report has arisen:
an3.65:1.7: and it is good to see such *arahants*.’”
an3.65:11.1: “And, Kālāmas, this hateful person, overcome by hatred, with mind [*citta*] possessed by it, kills living beings, takes what is not given, goes to another's wife, speaks falsely, and encourages another to do the same, which leads to his harm and *dukkha* for a long time.”
an3.65:13.2: when delusion arises within a person, does it arise for welfare or for harm?”
an3.65:15.1: “And, Kālāmas, this deluded person, overcome by delusion, with mind [*citta*] possessed by it, kills living beings, takes what is not given, goes to another's wife, speaks falsely, and encourages another to do the same, which leads to his harm and *dukkha* for a long time.”
an3.65:17.1: “What do you think, Kālāmas: are these *dhammas* wholesome or unwholesome?”
an3.65:2.1: Then the Kālāmas of Kesamutta approached the *Bhagavā*; having approached, some paid homage to the *Bhagavā* and sat down to one side; some exchanged greetings with the *Bhagavā*, and when the courteous and memorable conversation was finished, sat down to one side; some raised their joined palms toward the *Bhagavā* and sat down to one side; some announced their name and clan and sat down to one side; some kept silent and sat down to one side. Sitting to one side, those Kālāmas of Kesamutta said this to the *Bhagavā*:
an3.65:21.1: “Censured by the wise or praised by the wise?”
an3.65:23.1: “When undertaken and adopted, do they lead to harm and *dukkha*, or not?
an3.65:23.2: How does it appear to you here?”
an3.65:24.1: “When undertaken, *bhante*, and adopted, they lead to harm and *dukkha*.
an3.65:24.2: That is how it appears to us here.”
an3.65:25.3: Do not go by oral tradition, do not go by lineage, do not go by hearsay, do not go by a collection of texts, do not go by reasoning, do not go by inference, do not go by reflection on appearances, do not go by acceptance after pondering a view, do not go by seeming competence, and do not go by thinking, “The ascetic is our teacher.”
an3.65:25.4: When you, Kālāmas, know for yourselves:
an3.65:25.5: “These *dhammas* are unwholesome, these *dhammas* are blameworthy, these *dhammas* are censured by the wise, these *dhammas*, when undertaken and adopted, lead to harm and *dukkha*,” then, Kālāmas, you should abandon them,’
an3.65:25.6: what was said was said in reference to this.
an3.65:26.1: Come, Kālāmas, do not go by oral tradition, do not go by lineage, do not go by hearsay, do not go by a collection of texts, do not go by reasoning, do not go by inference, do not go by reflection on appearances, do not go by acceptance after pondering a view, do not go by seeming competence, and do not go by thinking, ‘The ascetic is our teacher.’
an3.65:26.2: When you, Kālāmas, know for yourselves:
an3.65:26.3: ‘These *dhammas* are wholesome, these *dhammas* are blameless, these *dhammas* are praised by the wise, these *dhammas*, when undertaken and adopted, lead to welfare and *sukha*,’ then, Kālāmas, you should enter upon them and dwell in them.
an3.65:27.2: when non-greed arises within a person, does it arise for welfare or for harm?”
an3.65:29.1: “And, Kālāmas, this non-greedy person, not overcome by greed, with mind [*citta*] not possessed by it, does not kill living beings, does not take what is not given, does not go to another's wife, does not speak falsely, and does not encourage another to do the same, which leads to his welfare and *sukha* for a long time.”
an3.65:3.1: “There are, *bhante*, some ascetics and brahmins who come to Kesamutta.
an3.65:3.2: They explain and illuminate only their own doctrine, but they disparage, denigrate, scorn, and tear down the doctrines of others.
an3.65:3.3: And there are also, *bhante*, some other ascetics and brahmins who come to Kesamutta.
an3.65:3.4: They too explain and illuminate only their own doctrine, but they disparage, denigrate, scorn, and tear down the doctrines of others.
an3.65:3.5: For us, *bhante*, there is indeed doubt, there is uncertainty:
an3.65:3.6: ‘Which of these venerable ascetics and brahmins speaks truth, and which speaks falsely?’”
an3.65:3.7: “It is enough for you to doubt, Kālāmas, enough for you to be uncertain.
an3.65:3.8: Your uncertainty has arisen in a matter that is indeed doubtful.
an3.65:31.2: when non-hatred arises within a person, does it arise …
an3.65:31.3: when non-delusion arises within a person, does it arise …
an3.65:33.1: “What do you think, Kālāmas: are these *dhammas* wholesome or unwholesome?”
an3.65:37.1: “Censured by the wise or praised by the wise?”
an3.65:39.1: “When undertaken and adopted, do they lead to welfare and *sukha*, or not?
an3.65:39.2: How does it appear to you here?”
an3.65:4.1: Come, Kālāmas, do not go by oral tradition, do not go by lineage, do not go by hearsay, do not go by a collection of texts, do not go by reasoning, do not go by inference, do not go by reflection on appearances, do not go by acceptance after pondering a view, do not go by seeming competence, and do not go by thinking, ‘The ascetic is our teacher.’
an3.65:4.2: When you, Kālāmas, know for yourselves:
an3.65:4.3: ‘These *dhammas* are unwholesome, these *dhammas* are blameworthy, these *dhammas* are censured by the wise, these *dhammas*, when undertaken and adopted, lead to harm and *dukkha*,’ then, Kālāmas, you should abandon them.
an3.65:40.1: “When undertaken, *bhante*, and adopted, they lead to welfare and *sukha*.
an3.65:40.2: That is how it appears to us here.”
an3.65:41.3: Do not go by oral tradition, do not go by lineage, do not go by hearsay, do not go by a collection of texts, do not go by reasoning, do not go by inference, do not go by reflection on appearances, do not go by acceptance after pondering a view, do not go by seeming competence, and do not go by thinking, “The ascetic is our teacher.”
an3.65:41.4: When you, Kālāmas, know for yourselves—
an3.65:41.5: these *dhammas* are wholesome, these *dhammas* are blameless, these *dhammas* are praised by the wise, these *dhammas*, when undertaken and adopted, lead to welfare and *sukha*, then, Kālāmas, you should enter upon them and dwell in them,’
an3.65:41.6: what was said was said in reference to this.
an3.65:42.1: Then, Kālāmas, that noble disciple, thus free of covetousness, free of ill will, undeluded, fully aware [*sampajāno*], and mindful, dwells having pervaded one direction with a mind [*cetasā*] accompanied by goodwill [*mettā*], likewise a second, likewise a third, likewise a fourth. Thus above, below, across, everywhere, and in every way, he dwells having pervaded the whole world with a mind [*cetasā*] accompanied by goodwill [*mettā*], abundant, exalted, immeasurable, without hostility, without ill will.
an3.65:42.2: With a mind [*cetasā*] accompanied by compassion [*karuṇā*] …
an3.65:42.3: with a mind [*cetasā*] accompanied by gladness [*muditā*] …
an3.65:42.4: he dwells having pervaded one direction with a mind [*cetasā*] accompanied by equanimity [*upekkhā*], likewise a second, likewise a third, likewise a fourth. Thus above, below, across, everywhere, and in every way, he dwells having pervaded the whole world with a mind [*cetasā*] accompanied by equanimity [*upekkhā*], abundant, exalted, immeasurable, without hostility, without ill will.
an3.65:43.1: Then, Kālāmas, that noble disciple has a mind [*citta*] thus without hostility, a mind [*citta*] thus without ill will, a mind [*citta*] thus undefiled, a mind [*citta*] thus purified.
an3.65:43.2: In this very life, four assurances are attained by him.
an3.65:43.3: ‘If there is another world, and there is fruit and result of good and bad *kammas*, then, with the breakup of the body after death, I will reappear in a good destination, a heavenly world.’ This is the first assurance attained by him.
an3.65:44.1: ‘But if there is no other world, and there is no fruit and result of good and bad *kammas*, then in this very life I maintain myself without hostility, without ill will, free from trouble, and happy.’ This is the second assurance attained by him.
an3.65:45.1: ‘But if evil is done by one who acts, I do not intend evil toward anyone.
an3.65:45.2: Since I do no evil *kamma*, from where will *dukkha* touch me?’ This is the third assurance attained by him.
an3.65:46.1: ‘But if evil is not done by one who acts, then I see myself purified in both respects.’ This is the fourth assurance attained by him.
an3.65:47.1: Then, Kālāmas, that noble disciple has a mind [*citta*] thus without hostility, a mind [*citta*] thus without ill will, a mind [*citta*] thus undefiled, a mind [*citta*] thus purified.
an3.65:47.2: In this very life, these four assurances are attained by him.”
an3.65:48.1: “So it is, *Bhagavā*, so it is, Fortunate One.
an3.65:48.2: That noble disciple, *bhante*, has a mind [*citta*] thus without hostility, a mind [*citta*] thus without ill will, a mind [*citta*] thus undefiled, a mind [*citta*] thus purified.
an3.65:48.3: In this very life, four assurances are attained by him.
an3.65:48.4: ‘If there is another world, and there is fruit and result of good and bad *kammas*, then, with the breakup of the body after death, I will reappear in a good destination, a heavenly world.’ This is the first assurance attained by him.
an3.65:49.1: ‘But if there is no other world, and there is no fruit and result of good and bad *kammas*, then in this very life I maintain myself without hostility, without ill will, free from trouble, and happy.’ This is the second assurance attained by him.
an3.65:5.2: when greed arises within a person, does it arise for welfare or for harm?”
an3.65:50.1: ‘But if evil is done by one who acts, I do not intend evil toward anyone; since I do no evil *kamma*, from where will *dukkha* touch me?’ This is the third assurance attained by him.
an3.65:51.1: ‘But if evil is not done by one who acts, then I see myself purified in both respects.’ This is the fourth assurance attained by him.
an3.65:52.1: That noble disciple, *bhante*, has a mind [*citta*] thus without hostility, a mind [*citta*] thus without ill will, a mind [*citta*] thus undefiled, a mind [*citta*] thus purified.
an3.65:52.2: In this very life, these four assurances are attained by him.
an3.65:53.2: we, *bhante*, go for refuge to the *Bhagavā*, and to the *dhamma*, and to the *saṅgha* of *bhikkhus*.
an3.65:53.3: May the *Bhagavā* remember us as lay followers who have gone for refuge from today onward, as long as life lasts.”
an3.65:7.1: “And, Kālāmas, this greedy person, overcome by greed, with mind [*citta*] possessed by it, kills living beings, takes what is not given, goes to another's wife, speaks falsely, and encourages another to do the same, which leads to his harm and *dukkha* for a long time.”
an3.65:9.2: when hatred arises within a person, does it arise for welfare or for harm?”
